text.title: Freelancer Positioning Content Calendar Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Freelancer Positioning Content Calendar. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: 3-5 pillars only. Fewer = brittle. More = unfocused.
rule.rationale: Outside 3-5 the rotation either repeats or sprawls.

rule.statement: Every week of the 12-month calendar is mapped to exactly one pillar.
Each topic must trace to a real client engagement, case study, or recurring question.
rule.rationale: Invented topics signal as inauthentic and underperform.

rule.statement: Minimum cadence: 1 published asset / week. Sub-weekly cadence loses signal.
rule.rationale: Sub-weekly cadence does not build the SEO + audience flywheel.

rule.statement: Owner is a named freelancer (yourself). 'Team' / 'agency' / blank not allowed.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership leads to skipped weeks.

rule.statement: Quarterly review: pillar fit, engagement per pillar, rotation drift. Calendar refreshes per quarter.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-freelancer-positioning-content-calendar.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
